questions: You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. Your team's project plan just got thrown out because the client changed the requirements two days before the deadline. How do you respond?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. You've been cooking the same recipe for a dinner party and realize halfway through that you're missing a key ingredient. What do you do?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. Your family's annual vacation destination is suddenly unavailable due to weather, and you need to pick somewhere new today. How do you handle it?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. You show up to give a presentation and discover the projector is broken and you need to present without slides. What's your approach?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. Your commute route is blocked by construction and you're running late for an important meeting. How do you react?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. You've been training for months in one position on the team, and the coach moves you to a completely different role mid-season. What do you do?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. Your professor changes the exam format from multiple choice to essay the week before the test. How do you respond?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. The restaurant you reserved for your anniversary is closed for a private event when you arrive. How do you handle the evening?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. Your company restructures and your department is merged with another, changing your role significantly. What's your reaction?

You're someone who thrives on routine and feels most comfortable when things go exactly as planned. You're leading a group hike and the trail is washed out, requiring a completely different route. How do you proceed?
